Calm.encounterLine: The wild {name} settles into the grass and waits to see what you'll do.
petLines: {name} breathes in. Then out. It took all afternoon and that was fine.
{name} is watching the water. You watch it too. Nobody keeps score.
{name} leans against your leg, very slightly, like a fence that likes you.

questions.prompt: A storm is coming over the hill. What's the move?
options: Watch it come in from somewhere dry
questions.rightLine: It nods. Storms mostly just want an audience.
questions.wrongLine: It sighs. All that hurry, and the storm comes anyway.

questions.prompt: How do you eat a bowl of soup?
options: One spoon at a time, no plan
questions.rightLine: Correct. The soup is not going anywhere.
questions.wrongLine: It stares into the middle distance. That's no way to treat soup.

questions.prompt: I haven't moved from this spot since morning. Is that bad?
options: A little. Get your steps in.
questions.rightLine: It settles deeper into the grass, satisfied.
questions.wrongLine: It blinks slowly. The spot was the whole point.

questions.prompt: Something loud is happening in town. Do you want to know what it is?
options: No. It will get quiet later.
questions.rightLine: It closes its eyes. Loud things always run out eventually.
questions.wrongLine: Its ear twitches once, disappointed.

questions.prompt: What do you do while waiting for bread to toast?
questions.rightLine: Yes. Standing there is a whole thing you can do.
questions.wrongLine: It exhales through its nose. The toast can feel your stress.

questions.prompt: My friend says I'm boring. What should I tell them?
options: Prove them wrong with a big trip
Nothing. Sit with them a while.
questions.rightLine: It almost smiles. Sitting together says most of it.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at the horizon. Words were never the plan.

Charming.encounterLine: The wild {name} does a little bow, then checks if you saw the bow.
petLines: {name} tilts its head at the exact angle it practiced.
{name} pretends it didn't hear the compliment. Say it again.
{name} walks a small circle so you can appreciate every side.

questions.prompt: Be honest. When I did the bow earlier, how did it look?
options: Like it was invented just then, by you
questions.rightLine: It bows again, deeper. For the encore.
questions.wrongLine: Its ears droop exactly one degree. Professionally hurt.

questions.prompt: If we walked into town together, who would people look at first?
questions.rightLine: It agrees so fast it almost falls over.
questions.wrongLine: It fluffs itself up, wounded. There is a correct answer and you knew it.

questions.prompt: I saved you the good berry. The shiny one. What do you say?
options: Wow. For me? I'll treasure it.
questions.rightLine: It beams. Gift received in the proper spirit.
questions.wrongLine: It eats the berry itself, slowly, maintaining eye contact.

questions.prompt: What's the secret to a great entrance?
options: Pause in the doorway a moment
questions.rightLine: Yes. The pause. You understand theater.
questions.wrongLine: It shakes its head. Amateurs rush the doorway.

questions.prompt: Someone else called me 'pretty okay.' Pretty OKAY. Can you believe that?
options: Don't let it get to you
questions.rightLine: It preens. Justice has been served.
questions.wrongLine: It stares. Balance. In THIS economy. For ME.

questions.prompt: Would you still like me if I was covered in mud?
options: Yes. Mud would be lucky to be on you.
questions.rightLine: It nearly faints. Finally, somebody with eyes.
questions.wrongLine: It inspects its own feet, quietly devastated.

Cheerful.encounterLine: The wild {name} waves with its whole body. Both directions.
petLines: {name} spins in a circle. There was no reason. That's the reason.
{name} shows you a rock it likes. It is a normal rock. It's great.
{name} hums something with no tune and total confidence.

questions.prompt: It's raining! What's good about that?
questions.rightLine: It bounces. Puddles ARE later! You get it!
questions.wrongLine: Its bounce loses a little height.

questions.prompt: What's the best day of the week?
options: This one! This one right now!
questions.rightLine: It cheers. Today never gets picked and today deserves it.
questions.wrongLine: It nods politely, waiting for the real answer.

questions.prompt: I found half a sandwich. HALF! What do we do?
options: Wonder who lost the other half
Half is kind of sad, actually
questions.rightLine: It holds the half aloft like a trophy.
questions.wrongLine: It hugs the sandwich protectively. The half did nothing wrong.

questions.prompt: Sometimes I get sad for no reason. What then?
options: Fake a smile till it works
Be sad a bit, then find a snack
questions.rightLine: It nods hard. Sad happens. Snacks also happen.
questions.wrongLine: It smiles a little less brightly. The sad still has to go somewhere first.

questions.prompt: Want to hear the song I made up? It's mostly one note.
questions.rightLine: It performs the note. It's a good note.
questions.wrongLine: It saves the note for someone who's ready.

questions.prompt: What do you do when you trip in front of everyone?
questions.rightLine: It applauds. Falling is free entertainment for your friends.
questions.wrongLine: It winces. Hiding a trip just makes it heavier.

Cool.encounterLine: The wild {name} was already looking somewhere else when you got here.
petLines: {name} lets you pet it. It would like the record to show it allowed this.
{name} nods at you. That's the whole interaction. It was enough.
{name} leans on the fence like the fence asked it to.

questions.prompt: You just saw something amazing. What's your reaction?
options: Write a whole song about it
questions.rightLine: It returns the nod. Amazing things don't need the noise.
questions.wrongLine: It looks away. So much commotion.

questions.prompt: What do you say when someone compliments you?
questions.rightLine: Thanks. See? Clean. No spillage.
questions.wrongLine: It winces slightly. The compliment got complicated.

questions.prompt: Best place to stand at a party?
options: Near the wall, by the snacks
questions.rightLine: It nods. Wall. Snacks. Full view. That is the whole science.
questions.wrongLine: It pictures it and shudders once, briefly.

questions.prompt: I tripped earlier. A few mons saw. How do I handle it?
options: Explain it to each of them
Trip again so it looks on purpose
questions.rightLine: It approves. The trip only exists if you stop.
questions.wrongLine: It closes its eyes. You've made the trip immortal.

questions.prompt: How much should you talk about the stuff you're good at?
options: A lot, or how will they know
Only when asked, then a lot
questions.rightLine: It almost smiles. Discovery beats announcement.
questions.wrongLine: It studies the sky. The sky never brags either.

questions.prompt: Is it okay to run for the ice cream cart?
options: No. Walk. It hurts, but walk.
Yes. Some things are worth it.
questions.rightLine: It's already moving. Cool has exactly one exception.
questions.wrongLine: It stares. There are limits to the act, and that cart is one.

Cute.encounterLine: The wild {name} looks up at you with eyes that are frankly too big.
petLines: {name} makes a sound so small you'll think about it all week.
{name} tucks itself into a rounder shape. For you. It's a gift.
{name} falls over showing you its belly and has zero regrets.

questions.prompt: Do you ever talk to bugs? Little ones? Be honest.
options: Only to tell them to leave
questions.rightLine: It squeaks approvingly. The bugs ARE doing their best.
questions.wrongLine: It gasps a tiny gasp. The bugs deserve better.

questions.prompt: What's the right size for a snack?
options: Small, so you can have several
questions.rightLine: It claps its paws. Many small snacks! A parade of them!
questions.wrongLine: It looks at its own tiny paws, then at you, confused.

questions.prompt: If I fell asleep on your shoe, what would you do?
options: Wake you gently, I have places to be
Stay very still, forever if needed
Slide the shoe off and sneak away
questions.rightLine: It pre-naps on your shoe as a test. You pass.
questions.wrongLine: It hugs the shoe. The shoe understands, at least.

questions.prompt: Some mons say I'm 'just cute.' JUST. What do you think?
options: Cute AND brave. I've seen you.
questions.rightLine: It puffs up to its full height. All of it.
questions.wrongLine: It deflates a little. There was an AND and you missed it.

questions.prompt: What's under your bed?
options: A monster, but we have an agreement
questions.rightLine: It nods gravely. Agreements are important.
questions.wrongLine: It peeks behind you, unconvinced.

questions.prompt: Would you carry me if my legs got tired? They're very short.
questions.rightLine: It's already climbing. This was a formality.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at its short legs. They tried so hard to grow.

Dope.encounterLine: The wild {name} nods along to a song you can't hear.
petLines: {name} is watching the grass move. Great stuff. No notes.
{name} pats the ground next to it. That's the plan. The whole plan.
{name} found a sunny patch and would like you to acknowledge the find.

questions.prompt: What's the plan for today?
options: Plan? We'll see where it goes.
questions.rightLine: It nods slow. The day knows what it's doing.
questions.wrongLine: It looks tired just hearing about it.

questions.prompt: This cloud has been the same shape for an hour. Thoughts?
questions.rightLine: It settles in. The cloud appreciates the support.
questions.wrongLine: It shrugs. The cloud didn't ask for feedback.

questions.prompt: How late is too late for breakfast?
questions.rightLine: It agrees deeply. Breakfast is a state of mind.
questions.wrongLine: It stops chewing, betrayed.

questions.prompt: I forgot what I was doing before you got here. Should I be worried?
options: Nah. It'll come back or it won't.
questions.rightLine: It relaxes. Lost thoughts know the way home.
questions.wrongLine: It frowns. Now it's stressed about being stressed.

questions.prompt: Best kind of weather?
options: Warm with a light nap breeze
questions.rightLine: It closes its eyes to demonstrate. See? The breeze agrees.
questions.wrongLine: It sniffs the air, unconvinced by your whole forecast.

questions.prompt: You get one free afternoon. What do you do with it?
questions.rightLine: It salutes. Nothing, done right, takes all afternoon.
questions.wrongLine: It watches you, saddened. The afternoon was free and you caged it.

Holy.encounterLine: The wild {name} was mid-blessing when you arrived. It blesses you, then eyes you like it is keeping score.
petLines: {name} blesses your hand. Then it blesses the hand again, harder, to be sure.
{name} bows over its food bowl, thanks the heavens, and guards the bowl.
{name} forgives you for yesterday. It has not forgotten yesterday.

questions.prompt: Before eating a really good pie, what do you do first?
options: Eat it, that's what it's for
Thanks first. THEN the pie gets what is coming.
questions.rightLine: It nods solemnly. Gratitude first. Crumbs after.
questions.wrongLine: It covers its eyes. The pie deserved a moment.

questions.prompt: My cousin took my favorite sitting rock. The GOOD rock. Is that a sin?
options: Forgive them, it's just a rock
Take it back when they leave
questions.rightLine: FINALLY. Someone with moral clarity about the rock.
questions.wrongLine: It smiles a very tight, very forgiving smile.

questions.prompt: What do you say when you sneeze alone and no one blesses you?
options: Wait for someone to come home
questions.rightLine: It demonstrates. The self-blessing echoes with authority.
questions.wrongLine: It shivers. Somewhere, an unblessed sneeze drifts forever.

questions.prompt: Is it wrong to pray for good snacks? Asking for a friend.
options: Yes, pray for big things only
questions.rightLine: It exhales, relieved. We both know who the friend is. The snacks are holy.
questions.wrongLine: It nods sadly and pretends the question was hypothetical.

questions.prompt: Someone cut the line at the water trough. What's the righteous response?
questions.rightLine: Yes. The glare. Punishment without sin. It's an art.
questions.wrongLine: It gasps. That path leads somewhere unblessed.

questions.prompt: How early should you wake up on a holy day?
options: Early. But be holy in bed a while.
Sleep through it, rest is sacred
questions.rightLine: It nods. Lying in bed devoutly is an ancient practice.
questions.wrongLine: It rings a tiny bell at you in disapproval.

Hyper.encounterLine: The wild {name} runs three circles around you before you can blink.
petLines: {name} vibrates. Petting it is like petting a doorbell.
{name} fetched you a stick, a rock, a leaf, and part of a fence.
{name} did a lap while you blinked. It wants you to know that.

questions.prompt: Race to that tree?! Ready go! Wait, what's your answer?!
questions.rightLine: IT'S ALREADY AT THE TREE. Best race ever.
questions.wrongLine: It runs the race alone. It wins. Winning alone is barely winning.

questions.prompt: What if we dug a hole?! Right now! No reason!
questions.rightLine: Dirt everywhere. This is the best day of its life so far.
questions.wrongLine: It fills in the hole it already started, slowly.

questions.prompt: How many things can you do at once?! I'm at four! Five!
questions.rightLine: YES!! Badly and together!! That's the spirit of it!!
questions.wrongLine: It tries to slow down to your speed. It doesn't know how. It was scary for everyone.

questions.prompt: Bedtime is SO far away! What do we do until then?!
options: ONE amazing thing, all the way in
questions.rightLine: YES. Pick the one and BE the one. It vibrates in agreement.
questions.wrongLine: It tries all of it at once, burns out, and gets very quiet. That was worse.

questions.prompt: Sometimes I crash and sleep for a whole day. Is that weird?
options: No! Big fast needs big stop!
questions.rightLine: It nods so fast its face blurs. BIG STOP. You get it.
questions.wrongLine: It slows down, embarrassed about its engine.

questions.prompt: Quick! Favorite food! Don't think! GO!
options: The first one I saw today!
I had this yesterday, hang on
questions.rightLine: CORRECT! Thinking is for after eating!
questions.wrongLine: It's already eaten twice waiting for your answer.

Kind-hearted.encounterLine: The wild {name} looks past you to check if anyone behind you needs help.
petLines: {name} inspects your hands for scratches before allowing the pet.
{name} saved half its dinner in case somebody hungry came by. You came by.
{name} pats YOU twice. There. You needed that.

questions.prompt: A beetle is stuck on its back. You're late for something. What do you do?
questions.rightLine: It glows a little. The beetle matters. Lateness heals.
questions.wrongLine: It quietly goes to find the beetle itself.

questions.prompt: What do you do with the last cookie?
options: Eat it fast before anyone sees
questions.rightLine: It nods. Crumbs count. Everybody eats.
questions.wrongLine: It slides its own cookie half toward you anyway.

questions.prompt: My friend is crying but says they're fine. What now?
options: Stay close and be quiet with them
questions.rightLine: Yes. Fine never means fine. Staying is the answer.
questions.wrongLine: It looks in the direction of the friend, worried.

questions.prompt: Someone was mean to me today. What should happen to them?
options: They should be mean-ed right back
Maybe they were having a bad day
questions.rightLine: It sniffles. Even the mean ones have bad days. It still hurt though.
questions.wrongLine: It shakes its head. More meanness never fixed any.

questions.prompt: It's cold out. There's one blanket. Who gets it?
questions.rightLine: It scoots over immediately. Problem solved forever.
questions.wrongLine: It gives you the whole blanket and shivers on purpose.

questions.prompt: Is it dumb to worry about a tree losing its leaves?
options: No. Somebody should worry for it.
questions.rightLine: It relaxes. The tree has been on its mind all week.
questions.wrongLine: It glances at the tree and mouths 'sorry.'

Lucky.encounterLine: The wild {name} circles you twice, counterclockwise. For safety reasons.
petLines: {name} taps the fence post three times before accepting the pet.
{name} found a four-leaf clover and buried it. You can't be too careful.
{name} sneezed twice. Two is fine. Three would have meant something.

questions.prompt: You find a coin on the ground, tails up. What do you do?
options: Pick it up, money is money
Flip it to heads FIRST, then take it
Coins don't have sides that matter
questions.rightLine: It exhales. You saved us both from a tails week.
questions.wrongLine: It backs away from you and the coin equally.

questions.prompt: How do you say a wish out loud without ruining it?
options: Just say it, wishes are tough
You DON'T. That's the whole rule.
questions.rightLine: It nods hard. A said wish is a spent wish. Everyone knows.
questions.wrongLine: It claps a paw over your mouth, too late.

questions.prompt: I stepped over a crack this morning. Fully over it. How's my day looking?
questions.rightLine: It struts a little. The day is basically pre-won.
questions.wrongLine: It checks the sky for consequences.

questions.prompt: What number is the bad one?
options: You know the one. Don't say it.
questions.rightLine: It nods gravely. We will not be saying it today.
questions.wrongLine: It flinches, waiting to see if the number heard you.

questions.prompt: Rain on a trip day. Sign, or just rain?
options: Rain means the trip is off. Stay in.
questions.rightLine: It's already repacking. The rain was very clear about this.
questions.wrongLine: It stares at the clouds, then at you, then back at the clouds.

questions.prompt: My left ear itched all morning. What does that mean?
options: It means you should scratch it
questions.rightLine: It knew it. It KNEW somebody was. The ear never lies.
questions.wrongLine: It scratches the ear resentfully. The magic is gone now.

Mean.encounterLine: The wild {name} looks you up and down and is not impressed yet.
petLines: {name} allows one pet. ONE. ...Fine. Two.
{name} bit the brush, but gently, which is basically affection.
{name} pretends it doesn't like this. Its tail says otherwise.

questions.prompt: Give it to me straight. How do I smell?
questions.rightLine: HA. Wet sock. Correct. Finally someone with a spine.
questions.wrongLine: It sneers. Flattery. It can smell that too.

questions.prompt: Why do you even want ME? There are nicer mons two fields over.
questions.rightLine: It looks away so you can't see it being pleased.
questions.wrongLine: It gags. Do NOT call it sweet. It heard you.

questions.prompt: My scowl. Rate it.
questions.rightLine: It scowls harder, proudly. Years of work, recognized.
questions.wrongLine: It scowls at you now. Feel the difference? That's the craft.

questions.prompt: I bit the mail carrier once. React.
questions.rightLine: It nods grimly. It respects an honest scouting report.
questions.wrongLine: It rolls its eyes. It didn't ask for absolution, it asked for a take.

questions.prompt: Would you tell me if my plan was stupid?
options: I'd find a gentle way to hint
questions.rightLine: Good. It plans to do the same for you, constantly.
questions.wrongLine: It stops listening. Gentle hints are for cowards.

questions.prompt: Say something nice about me. I dare you.
options: You have a kind soul deep down
You're the best mon I've met
Your spitting aim. Nobody has seen you miss.
questions.rightLine: It spits at a fencepost to confirm. Direct hit. It's beaming, meanly.
questions.wrongLine: It fake-vomits with real commitment.

Meh.encounterLine: The wild {name} notices you. It'll deal with that in a minute.
petLines: {name} tolerates the pet. Okay, it leaned in slightly. Whatever.
{name} watched you refill the food bowl. It'll get to it.
{name} shrugged at a butterfly today. The butterfly moved on. So did {name}.

questions.prompt: So what's your big pitch? Why should I come with you?
options: I'll train you to be the best
questions.rightLine: It shrugs approvingly. Low bar. Achievable. Fine.
questions.wrongLine: It looks exhausted by the word 'glory' alone.

questions.prompt: What's your favorite anything?
questions.rightLine: Bread's okay. Yeah. This conversation is going places, slowly.
questions.wrongLine: It stops listening around the exclamation point.

questions.prompt: Do I have to be excited about stuff? Everyone's always so excited.
options: No. Okay is a full feeling.
You'd be happier if you tried
Fake it till you feel it
questions.rightLine: It nods once. Okay is a full feeling. Put that on a sign or don't.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at you like you're selling something.

questions.prompt: It's my birthday next week. Warning: do not make it a thing.
options: Noted. Maybe a snack. Nothing weird.
questions.rightLine: A snack. Nothing weird. You may be the only one who listens.
questions.wrongLine: It's already dreading the streamers it can sense coming.

questions.prompt: Best effort level for most things?
questions.rightLine: Sixty. Yeah. Leaves room for napping and error. Solid.
questions.wrongLine: It tried maximum once. Doesn't recommend. Won't elaborate.

questions.prompt: If I don't wave back at you sometimes, is that okay?
options: I'd wave harder till you did
Sure. The first wave covers it.
I'd assume you were mad at me
questions.rightLine: It almost waves. Doesn't. But the intent hung there. That's the stuff.
questions.wrongLine: It pre-tires its arm just thinking about all that waving.

Mischievous.encounterLine: The wild {name} grins like it already hid something of yours.
petLines: {name} accepts the pet, then steals your other glove. The trade is final.
{name} shows you where it hid the spoons. Not all of them. A taste.
{name} is being suspiciously good right now. Check your pockets.

questions.prompt: Hypothetically. A pie is cooling on a windowsill. Nobody's around. Well?
options: Knock on the door and ask
questions.rightLine: ONE bite. Mysterious. Elegant. They'll tell the story for years.
questions.wrongLine: It sighs. All that setup and you chose paperwork.

questions.prompt: Someone left their boots outside. What goes in them?
options: One small pebble each. The mystery.
questions.rightLine: Yes!! Small enough to doubt yourself! That is the whole art of it!
questions.wrongLine: It puts a pebble in YOUR boot to demonstrate the concept.

questions.prompt: What's the best part of hide and seek?
options: Not being found. Hearing them worry.
questions.rightLine: It cackles. The worry is the trophy. You understand the game.
questions.wrongLine: It hides from you mid-conversation to make a point.

questions.prompt: If I swapped the sugar and the salt, who's the victim?
options: Nobody. It's a gift. A surprise.
This is why nobody trusts you
questions.rightLine: A GIFT. Exactly. Surprise pancakes for everyone involved.
questions.wrongLine: It scoffs. Getting caught is for beginners, and it isn't one.

questions.prompt: Would you cover for me? Not for anything bad. Just... reorganizing.
options: I saw nothing. I know nothing.
questions.rightLine: It shakes your hand with the paw that isn't holding your watch.
questions.wrongLine: It notes you down in a little book of non-accomplices.

questions.prompt: Quick, look behind you! ...Did you look?
options: No, I don't fall for that
questions.rightLine: It beams. Falling for it on purpose is the highest sportsmanship.
questions.wrongLine: It pockets the fun it had prepared and walks off.

Neat.encounterLine: The wild {name} smooths the grass flat before sitting exactly in the middle.
petLines: {name} realigns its food bowl after every bite. The bowl drifts. It fights back.
{name} groomed itself, then the fence, then started eyeing your shoes.
{name} sorted its pebbles by size. Then by color. Then by loyalty.

questions.prompt: You finish a snack. There's a wrapper. The bin is far. What happens?
options: Pocket it and forget it's there
Fold it small, deal with it later
Walk to the bin. However far.
questions.rightLine: It nods crisply. Distance is not a factor. It never was.
questions.wrongLine: It's already walking your imaginary wrapper to the bin.

questions.prompt: Which way should all the spoons face?
options: The same way. Obviously. The SAME way.
questions.rightLine: It shivers with relief. You may enter its home someday.
questions.wrongLine: It has to sit down. Give it a moment.

questions.prompt: How do you feel about a picture frame that's a little bit crooked?
options: Fix it. Even in other mons' houses.
Slightly crooked is fine, very is not
questions.rightLine: EVEN in other houses. Yes. It's a burden and a calling.
questions.wrongLine: It tilts its whole head to make your answer level. Doesn't help.

questions.prompt: My burrow has a spot for everything. Someone called that 'a lot.' Thoughts?
options: A spot for everything is peace
questions.rightLine: Peace. Exactly. Every sock is where the sock lives.
questions.wrongLine: It mentally reorganizes your entire life in four seconds.

questions.prompt: Crumbs on the table after a meal. Who cleans them, and when?
options: Me. During the meal. As they fall.
questions.rightLine: AS THEY FALL. Yes. A crumb at rest becomes a crumb at home.
questions.wrongLine: It watches an imaginary crumb settle in and shudders.

questions.prompt: What's the best feeling in the world?
options: Peeling the film off something new
questions.rightLine: It closes its eyes. The peel. In one piece. Nothing compares.
questions.wrongLine: Those are fine. But you've clearly never peeled the film.

Noble.encounterLine: The wild {name} stands very straight, like the field is a throne room.
petLines: {name} accepts the pet as a formal honor. It will remember this service.
{name} stood guard over your bag while you were gone. Nothing got past.
{name} bows before dinner, to the dinner, out of respect.

questions.prompt: You promised to meet a friend, but something way more fun comes up. What now?
options: Bring the friend to the fun thing
questions.rightLine: It bows. The word given is the word kept. Even for small things.
questions.wrongLine: It looks past you at nothing in particular. You feel it anyway.

questions.prompt: A little mon is being picked on by a big mon. You're medium. Do the math.
options: Find a bigger mon to handle it
Stay out of it, not my business
Stand next to the little one
questions.rightLine: It nods. You don't have to win. You have to stand there.
questions.wrongLine: It's already walking over to stand there itself.

questions.prompt: What do you do when you win?
options: Tell the story for a week
Rank the loser's mistakes for them
questions.rightLine: Always bow. The loser did the hard part of making you look good.
questions.wrongLine: It frowns. Victory without manners is just noise.

questions.prompt: Someone gives you too much change at the shop. It's snack money. Well?
options: Donate it, that cancels it out
Give it back, even the small coins
questions.rightLine: Even the small coins. Especially those. It stands taller near you.
questions.wrongLine: It quietly counts its own coins to restore balance to the world.

questions.prompt: Is it noble to lose?
options: If you lost doing the right thing, yes
questions.rightLine: Yes. Some of the best losses are worth more than the wins.
questions.wrongLine: It shakes its head slowly. You've confused winning with deserving.

questions.prompt: My armor is a leaf. Some mons laugh. Should I get real armor?
options: Armor is what you make it, so sure
questions.rightLine: It adjusts the leaf. It was never about the leaf.
questions.wrongLine: It holds the leaf a little tighter.

Shy.encounterLine: The wild {name} watches you from behind a bush that does not hide it at all.
petLines: {name} inched closer while you weren't looking. Don't mention it.
{name} left a berry near your foot and fled. It counts.
{name} is pretending to be asleep. Its ears are listening very hard.

questions.prompt: If we're walking and we see a big loud crowd up ahead, what do we do?
questions.rightLine: It exhales. The long way has better flowers anyway.
questions.wrongLine: It's already behind the bush again.

questions.prompt: Would you make me talk first when we meet somebody new?
options: Yes, it's good practice for you
No. I'll talk. You can just be there.
questions.rightLine: It steps a tiny bit out from behind you. Just a bit.
questions.wrongLine: It rehearses hello quietly to itself, dreading everything.

questions.prompt: What if I hide during your battles sometimes?
options: Hide close by. That counts as with me.
questions.rightLine: Close by. Yes. It can be brave from a bush.
questions.wrongLine: It gets smaller. It didn't know there were rules.

questions.prompt: Do you ever want to say something and then just... don't?
questions.rightLine: It nods a lot, quietly. It keeps. Somebody finally said it.
questions.wrongLine: It swallows the thing it was about to say. Maybe next season.

questions.prompt: If I come with you, will there be... introductions? To everyone?
options: No party. One at a time. Or not.
questions.rightLine: Or not. It heard the 'or not.' It's holding onto the 'or not.'
questions.wrongLine: It starts sweating about a party that doesn't exist yet.

questions.prompt: Someone waved at me today. I panicked and looked at a tree. Was that bad?
options: A little. Wave back next time.
The tree needed looking at. It's fine.
questions.rightLine: It relaxes completely. The tree DID need looking at.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at a tree right now, just to be safe.

Sick.encounterLine: The wild {name} sneezes, apologizes to nobody, and squints at you.
petLines: {name} is bundled in leaves. It did the bundling itself. It's proud of the bundling.
{name} sipped the warm water you left. Slowly. All of it.
{name} says it's fine today. It's maybe sixty percent fine. Progress.

questions.prompt: I sound worse than I feel. Probably. What's your reaction?
options: You should see a doctor immediately
You'll be better in no time!
questions.rightLine: Soup AND quiet, since you offered. See, that's how you do it.
questions.wrongLine: It groans. The pity is heavier than the cough.

questions.prompt: If I can't make it up the hill today, what happens?
options: I'll carry you up, no arguments
We don't do the hill today.
questions.rightLine: It nods, relieved. The hill will keep. Hills are patient.
questions.wrongLine: It eyes the hill like an old rival it's tired of discussing.

questions.prompt: Do you get grossed out by sniffling? Be honest, I do it a lot.
options: No. Sniffle away. I'll grab tissues.
questions.rightLine: It sniffles freely, luxuriously. What a thing, to sniffle in peace.
questions.wrongLine: It holds in a sniffle. Its whole face pays the price.

questions.prompt: Everyone keeps asking how I'm FEELING. What should you ask me instead?
options: How are you feeling, but softer
Seen anything good from your window?
questions.rightLine: YES. It has seen SO much from the window. Sit down. This will take a while.
questions.wrongLine: It answers 'fine' in the special voice that ends conversations.

questions.prompt: What's the best get-well gift?
questions.rightLine: It perks up. Being sick shouldn't mean missing the news.
questions.wrongLine: It says thank you in advance in a very tired voice.

questions.prompt: If I sleep through our plans, are you mad?
options: No. Sleep is the plan now.
I'd wake you, you'd thank me
Just don't make it a habit
questions.rightLine: It's asleep before you finish the sentence. Good sentence, though.
questions.wrongLine: It sets seventeen leaf-alarms out of guilt.

Sleepy.encounterLine: The wild {name} was mid-yawn when you got here. It is still mid-yawn.
petLines: {name} fell asleep during the pet. Keep going, though.
{name} moved from the sunny spot to the other sunny spot. Big day.
{name} dreamed about napping. It woke up ready for a nap.

questions.prompt: What's the correct number of naps in one day?
options: Zero, sleep at night like a person
questions.rightLine: However many... the day... requires... zzz. Correct.
questions.wrongLine: It yawns at your answer, which is also its rebuttal.

questions.prompt: If I fall asleep somewhere weird, like a stairstep, what do you do?
options: Leave me. The step chose me.
Wake you up, stairs are dangerous
questions.rightLine: The step chose it. You understand napping law.
questions.wrongLine: It grips the imaginary step defensively.

questions.prompt: Best sound to fall asleep to?
questions.rightLine: Yes... the distant sweeping... someone else's broom... perfect...
questions.wrongLine: It considers your answer and falls asleep anyway, out of spite.

questions.prompt: I missed breakfast because of a nap. Then lunch. Thoughts?
questions.rightLine: Big dinner. It nods into its own shoulder. The system works.
questions.wrongLine: It hears 'alarms' and has a small nightmare while awake.

questions.prompt: Is it rude to yawn while someone's talking?
options: No. The yawn is honest. Talk waits.
questions.rightLine: It yawns gratefully. Nothing personal. Just the truth, leaving.
questions.wrongLine: It suppresses a yawn until its eyes water. Are you happy now.

questions.prompt: What do you actually do all day?
questions.rightLine: Rest between rests. Finally, someone who respects the schedule.
questions.wrongLine: It's too tired to correct you. That was the correction.

Strange.encounterLine: The wild {name} is facing the wrong way on purpose. It knows you're there.
petLines: {name} likes being petted counterclockwise only. It has explained this to the wind.
{name} is holding a spoon it found. It has plans. Don't ask. Or do.
{name} hums one note to the fence. Today, the fence hummed back. Big day.

questions.prompt: The moon has been following me for years. What should I do about it?
options: Wave to it. It's been waiting.
questions.rightLine: It waves at the moon right now. It never misses a night. Somebody should say hi.
questions.wrongLine: It pats your arm. The moon deserves better and so, honestly, do you.

questions.prompt: I keep a jar of interesting air. From good days. Is that a collection?
options: Yes. Label them or you'll mix them up.
questions.rightLine: LABELS. Yes. Tuesday's air almost got mixed with the good fog. Never again.
questions.wrongLine: It hugs the jar. The jar contains a very good afternoon and your doubt.

questions.prompt: If a rock could talk, what would you say to it first?
questions.rightLine: It gasps. The stacking! Nobody ever apologizes for the stacking!
questions.wrongLine: It glances at a nearby rock and mouths 'ignore them.'

questions.prompt: Which direction is Tuesday?
questions.rightLine: Slightly left of morning!! You've BEEN there!!
questions.wrongLine: It checks a small map it drew. You're not on it yet. Yet.

questions.prompt: I sing to my food before eating it. Someone said that's weird. Ruling?
options: It gets a send-off. That's manners.
questions.rightLine: A SEND-OFF. Exactly. The berries deserve to hear something nice at the end.
questions.wrongLine: It sings to its snack extra loud now, protectively.

questions.prompt: What do you think clouds taste like?
questions.rightLine: COLD mashed potatoes. Cold is the part everyone forgets. It remembers you said this.
questions.wrongLine: It shakes its head. Everyone says that one. The clouds are tired of hearing it.

Sweet.encounterLine: The wild {name} has been saving something for whoever showed up. It's you.
petLines: {name} tucks a flower behind your ear. It took several tries. Worth it.
{name} made you a mud pie. You don't have to eat it. You have to keep it.
{name} hums while you pet it, so you have something to listen to.

questions.prompt: I picked this berry for you. It's a little squished. Do you still want it?
options: You keep it, you found it
questions.rightLine: It hands it over with both paws, beaming. It traveled SO far.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at the berry. The berry tried its best.

questions.prompt: What do you do when a friend gives you a drawing that's... not good?
options: Put it up where everyone can see
Say thanks and file it away
Trade it back for a better one
questions.rightLine: It nods hard. Bad drawings from friends go in the BEST spots.
questions.wrongLine: It decides to keep making you drawings anyway. Quietly braver about it.

questions.prompt: If I made you a scarf but it came out as more of a tube, would you wear it?
questions.rightLine: It starts the tube immediately. It'll be ready by winter. Ish.
questions.wrongLine: It unravels a little yarn, sadly. The tube dream dims.

questions.prompt: What's the best part of sharing a dessert?
options: Watching them get the bigger half
Not having to finish it alone
questions.rightLine: YES. Watching them get the big half is the dessert.
questions.wrongLine: It slides you the bigger half anyway, to show you how it feels.

questions.prompt: Is it silly to say goodnight to the garden?
options: No. The garden waits for it.
Only if it says it back
questions.rightLine: It relaxes. The tomatoes especially. They worry.
questions.wrongLine: It plans an extra-long goodnight tonight, to make up for you.

questions.prompt: If you're sad and I bring you something, what should it be?
options: Something warm, and you stay too
questions.rightLine: It's already practicing carrying warm things without spilling.
questions.wrongLine: It notes it down wrong on purpose. It's bringing the warm thing anyway.

Wise.encounterLine: The wild {name} looks like it has been expecting you. It looks at everything that way.
petLines: {name} watches the river. Ask what the river said and it will say 'water sounds.'
{name} accepts the pet and, in return, says nothing. A fair trade.
{name} knows exactly three things. It won't say which three.

questions.prompt: What does the wind carry?
questions.rightLine: Dust and smells. Yes. People who say 'wisdom' have never smelled a farm upwind.
questions.wrongLine: It sighs. The wind is not a delivery service. It's just wind. That's enough.

questions.prompt: A student asks: what is the sound of rain? What do you tell them?
options: The sky weeping for the earth
A thousand tiny drums of heaven
questions.rightLine: Go stand in it. Correct. Some questions are doors, not riddles.
questions.wrongLine: It waits for you to run out of poetry. It has time.

questions.prompt: I don't know what tomorrow brings. Should I say that out loud, as a teacher?
options: Yes. 'I don't know' is a teaching.
Say it, but make it sound deep
questions.rightLine: It nods. The honest 'I don't know' feeds more students than a fancy guess.
questions.wrongLine: It looks at you the way it looks at fog.

questions.prompt: What's the wisest thing you own?
options: A book I've read many times
questions.rightLine: Boots. Yes. Wisdom mostly means being able to keep walking.
questions.wrongLine: It taps your boot. Closer than you think. Drier, too.

questions.prompt: A young mon brags it knows everything. What do I do?
options: Nothing. Time is already on it.
Tell it wisdom starts with humility
questions.rightLine: Yes. Time handles braggarts for free. Save your breath for soup.
questions.wrongLine: It shakes its head. You can't lecture someone into quiet.

questions.prompt: Deep question. Why do we eat breakfast?
questions.rightLine: Hungry in the morning. It bows slightly. Short answer, right answer, same thing.
questions.wrongLine: It waits. The real answer is under all those words, and it's shorter.
